what_it_does: Renders a compact dropdown chip for choosing a projection/lens binding. It shows a short label plus a live count, toggles an animated menu of all bindings, highlights the current one, and notifies the parent via onPick when a binding is selected.
observations: Merges lens and glance into a single text-minimal chip.
shortLabel truncates labels at the first em dash, en dash, or hyphen.
The live count has title and aria-label explaining 'things on the map'.
Uses AnimatePresence and motion.ul for menu entrance/exit.
Includes a scrim overlay to close the menu.
Uses a custom useChipMenu hook for open state, ref, and positioning class.
Applies stamp() data attributes to mark UI elements with stable URIs.
encodeURIComponent is used when stamping each binding id.
The caret character changes based on open state and is aria-hidden.
standouts: Accessibility annotations: aria-expanded, title/aria-label on the count, aria-hidden caret.
UI instrumentation via stamp() on the wrapper, current button, and each menu item.
Operator-law comment explicitly flags that a bare count needs human meaning.
conventions: React functional component with explicit TypeScript props.
Framer Motion for enter/exit animations using a shared motion token.
Custom hook encapsulates menu state, ref, and positioning class.
Stable UI identifiers via a stamp utility.
concerns: shortLabel splits on any hyphen/en dash/em dash, which may over-truncate labels where dashes are meaningful.
encodeURIComponent in stamp ids may produce unusual code_id values; stamp contract is unclear from this file.
No visible keyboard handling (escape, focus trap, arrow keys) in this file; may live in useChipMenu.
The count's visible text has no unit suffix; meaning depends on hover/screen reader.
notes: A compact, instrumented lens selector with an animated dropdown and accessibility annotations.
questions: What contract does stamp() expect for its URI argument, and is encodeURIComponent safe?
How does useChipMenu handle focus management, keyboard escape, and collision-aware positioning?
Is shortLabel's aggressive dash splitting intended for every registry label?
Should the visible count include a unit suffix rather than relying on title/aria-label?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: a merged, text-minimal chip showing a short name and live count, with full labels available in an on-demand menu.
apparent_themes: Minimal visible surface with rich hidden metadata.
Operator clarity for raw numeric counts.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give operators a compact, always-visible lens control that surfaces the active projection and its count while keeping the full set of choices one click away in an animated, accessible menu.
novelty: The combination of operator-law commentary about human-meaningful counts and stamp-based UI path instrumentation within a small dropdown chip.
contribution: A reusable, accessible, instrumented chip component that bridges a projection registry to user selection, preserving active-state highlighting, motion feedback, and stable UI markers.
summary_for_embedding: LensChip.tsx exports a React component that renders an animated dropdown chip showing the active projection's short label and live count, with a menu of all bindings and selection handling.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a compact, accessible control for switching map projections or lenses without hardcoded labels.

symbols.does: Derives a short display label by taking the substring before the first dash or em/en dash.
symbols.description: Splits the input label on em dash, en dash, or hyphen, trims whitespace, and returns the head; falls back to the full label if the head is empty.

symbols.does: Renders a compact dropdown chip for selecting a projection binding.
symbols.description: Displays the current projection's short label and live count in a button, toggles an animated menu of all bindings, highlights the active binding, and calls onPick when a binding is chosen; uses useChipMenu for open/close state and positioning, and stamps UI elements with stable identifiers.
